title: padus virginiana
intro: Padus virginiana, also known as American dogwood or cornel, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Padus virginiana include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, sore throats, and digestive problems.
